campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council met on March 24, 2026, to honor K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward on several items including an updated beekeeping ordinance, a study about creating homeless services in West Valley, and a major development bill affecting areas near transit zones.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial - police department's first working dog
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
Senate Bill 79 - transit-oriented development proposal with property conflict recusals
West Valley homeless services feasibility study and regional task force recommendation
Fiscal Year 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met with five different commissions to review and approve their plans and priorities for the next fiscal year. These commissions handle everything from maintaining local history and libraries to managing parks, roads, and development in Saratoga. The council also discussed a legal case in a private session.
keyTopics: Heritage Preservation Commission priorities for protecting historic sites
Library and Public Art Commission plans for community spaces and programs
Parks and Recreation Commission goals for local parks and activities
Traffic Safety Commission work on road safety improvements
Planning Commission agenda for new development and zoning decisions

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes and financial reports, while also addressing practical matters like weed control and affordable housing. The council also made plans to raise flags for Pride Month and Juneteenth, heard from the county transportation authority, and discussed their upcoming housing goals.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings
County transportation authority update (VTA)
New housing development implementation schedule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and move forward with several projects including a new residential development called Solana and updates to the general plan for housing. The council also discussed plans for community health and senior services for the year ahead.
keyTopics: Approved final map for Solana Project (Tract 10657) - new development moving forward
Reviewed January 2026 financial and investment report
Accepted annual progress report on General Plan and Housing Element
Approved 2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan
Updated council meeting minute-taking procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize upcoming council sessions. The council reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 7 meeting, then looked ahead to draft items for the April 14 meeting, making decisions about which topics to discuss and when to schedule them.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Set specific times for certain agenda items to keep meetings on track
Reviewed public records from late March 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for different city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling of board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal Community Development Block Grant funding, which helps pay for affordable housing and community services in our city. They also approved minutes from their previous meeting in February.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for affordable housing projects
Community development and human services funding decisions
Commission meeting minutes approval from February 25, 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council discussed ways to support local businesses and encourage shopping in Cupertino. They covered a business license amnesty program to help shops get compliant, plans to promote local shopping through summer events, and potential tax breaks or other incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to the area.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping shops catch up on licenses without penalties
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - promoting local businesses through community events
City partnerships with private companies to sponsor events and fund programs
Economic incentives for new retail stores, small businesses, and tech startups
Outreach to shopping centers to increase participation in city programs

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding a Historical and Landmarks Commission Hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city will discuss matters related to historic buildings and landmarks in the community. Residents can attend in person or join online via Zoom, and can submit written comments by noon on the day of the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission Hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline: noon on meeting day
Remote participation via Zoom or phone

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine administrative meeting on March 17, 2026, to handle scheduling and housekeeping items. The council approved meeting minutes, reviewed upcoming agenda items, and authorized Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 Annual conference. They also confirmed a school district representative appointment and gave the City Manager authority to negotiate an unspecified contract or agreement.
keyTopics: Mayor Montano travel approval for WELL 2026 conference
School district representative appointment to Economic committee
Approval of previous meeting minutes and meeting calendar
City Manager given negotiation authority for upcoming contract

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two major agreements. The council authorized a facility-sharing deal with the American Red Cross for emergency disaster relief and approved a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software (Mimecast) costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Disaster relief facility agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting packet
